excerpt: Ibrāhīm traversed the first two degrees: he possessed ʿilm al-yaqīn through revelation, and then attained ʿayn al-yaqīn through witnessing the miracle.
Had Jesus possessed greater perfection in his certainty, he would have walked upon the air itself.
source_author: The Prophet Muḥammad, may Allāh's blessings and peace be upon him
source_location_hint: Referenced in relation to degrees of yaqīn

excerpt: Even if all the veils that conceal the universe were torn away from before me, my certainty would not increase by even the smallest measure (Lokashf al-Ghita ma eternity).
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: Chapter title and repeated throughout KASHKOLE

excerpt: The Qur'ān, its rulings, and all the knowledge of the outward and inward workings of this universe stand perpetually before my eyes.
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib in dialogue with ʿUmar
source_location_hint: Historical anecdote of ʿAlī and ʿUmar's discourse

excerpt: The Prophet Sulaymān, peace be upon him, was my disciple, and I taught him all the languages—those of jinn, beasts, birds, and dragons.
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: Anecdote of the pigeons in Kūfa mosque

excerpt: When Allāh created me, He asked my name and identity. A righteous one in the world of light taught me the proper response. It is thus that ʿAlī named me.
source_work: Attributed to Jibrīl (Gabriel) via ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib

excerpt: When I looked upward at the heavens, my gaze pierced through the veils and reached the Upper Throne. When I gazed upon the earth, my sight traversed through all its layers. When I turned toward the east and the west and could not find Gabriel anywhere, I understood that it was he.
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib

excerpt: With one blessed finger, he struck the earthen wall beside him, and by his word, the wall transformed into gleaming gold. Then he said 'Return to earth' and it reverted to clay.
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: Narrative of the wall and the Jewish merchant

excerpt: Approaching the corpse, ʿAlī said: 'Rise up, by the leave of Allāh, O Madrik ibn Anzal ibn Salama!' The young man rose and cried out: 'Here am I! Here am I, O proof of Allāh upon the earth!'
source_location_hint: Narrative of the victim's resurrection in Kūfa mosque

excerpt: The Holy Prophet descended in a cluster of angels. The Prophet said to Abu Bakr: 'This is ʿAlī's place, the place most exalted!' ʿAlī stood in tears, saying: 'How I long that I could journey with you to that high destination!'
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_author: ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib and the Prophet Muḥammad
source_location_hint: Narrative of the Prophet's return to the mosque

excerpt: I was one of the many jinn in the time of Nūḥ. When the Prophet Nūḥ was boarding the Ark, I began to drill a hole in it. Then ʿAlī came upon me and struck my arm with his sword such force that my limb was severed. Since that day, I have lived in great terror of him.

ʿAlī fought the jinn in three ways: by burning them, by reciting Qur'ānic verses, and by clothing them in human form and slaying them with his sword. No other Imām save him could have performed this deed. Those who remain shall be reckoned with by the sword of al-Qāʾim.
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib

excerpt: ʿAlī revealed to Salmān that he had been his rescuer in the desert at ʿArzān, producing the very flower that Salmān had given to the horseman as proof of his transcendence beyond time and age.
source_work: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: Narrative of Salmān al-Fārisī on the rooftop

excerpt: When Imām al-Mustaʿlī commanded the tiger to seize Nizār, it pounced upon him, but he hid behind a door and the guards controlled the beast. Thus the Imām demonstrated his authority over all creation.
source_location_hint: Narrative of Nizār's challenge and refutation

excerpt: The Imām commanded a lion depicted on a carpet to emerge and consume the magician. The lion devoured him and returned to become a picture. When asked to restore the magician, the Imām replied that truth does not regurgitate falsehood.
source_location_hint: Narrative of the magician's defeat at the Caliph's banquet

excerpt: The Imām commanded the man to scratch his back three times. First it was covered with boils, then perfectly clean, then invisible—thereby demonstrating his dominion over matter and form itself.

When a sincere believer prayed to Imām al-Ḥākim for aid against three men who insulted the Imām, each attacker saw the Imām's form in the other and they killed one another. The Imām then appeared to say: 'You remembered me with sincerity, so I came to you!'
source_location_hint: Narrative of the three insulters in the mosque
